content:
# Codex CLI Reference and Features

## 1. Features
Overview of functionality in the Codex terminal client

Codex supports workflows beyond chat. Use this guide to learn what each one unlocks and when to use it.

### Running in interactive mode
Codex launches into a full-screen terminal UI that can read your repository, make edits, and run commands as you iterate together. Use it whenever you want a conversational workflow where you can review Codex's actions in real time.

```bash
codex
```

You can also specify an initial prompt on the command line.

```bash
codex "Explain this codebase to me"
```

Once the session is open, you can:
- Send prompts, code snippets, or screenshots directly into the composer.
- Watch Codex explain its plan before making a change, and approve or reject steps inline.
- Read syntax-highlighted markdown code blocks and diffs in the TUI, then use `/theme` to preview and save a preferred theme.
- Use `/clear` to wipe the terminal and start a fresh chat, or press `Ctrl + L` to clear the screen without starting a new conversation.
- Use `/copy` to copy the latest completed Codex output.
- Navigate draft history in the composer with `Up / Down`.
- Press `Ctrl + C` or use `/exit` to close the interactive session.

### Resuming conversations
Codex stores your transcripts locally so you can pick up where you left off.

- `codex resume` launches a picker of recent interactive sessions.
- `codex resume --all` shows sessions beyond the current working directory.
- `codex resume --last` skips the picker and jumps straight to your most recent session from the current working directory.
- `codex resume <SESSION_ID>` targets a specific run.

Non-interactive automation runs can resume too:
```bash
codex exec resume --last "Fix the race conditions you found"
codex exec resume 7f9f9a2e-1b3c-4c7a-9b0e-.... "Implement the plan"
```

### Models and reasoning
For most tasks, `gpt-5.4` is the recommended model. Switch models mid-session with the `/model` command

odex exec resume 7f9f9a2e-1b3c-4c7a-9b0e-.... "Implement the plan"
```

### Models and reasoning
For most tasks, `gpt-5.4` is the recommended model. Switch models mid-session with the `/model` command, or specify one when launching:
```bash
codex --model gpt-5.4
```

### Feature flags
Use the `features` subcommand to inspect and persist changes in your configuration.
```bash
codex features list
codex features enable unified_exec
codex features disable shell_snapshot
```

### Subagents
Use Codex subagent workflows to parallelize larger tasks. Configure roles in `[agents]` in `config.toml`.

### Image inputs
Attach screenshots or design specs:
```bash
codex -i screenshot.png "Explain this error"
codex --image img1.png,img2.jpg "Summarize these diagrams"
```

### Syntax highlighting and themes
Use `/theme` to open the theme picker. Custom `.tmTheme` files can be added under `$CODEX_HOME/themes`.

### Running local code review
Type `/review` in the CLI to open review presets:
- Review against a base branch.
- Review uncommitted changes.
- Review a commit.
- Custom review instructions.

### Web search
Codex enables web search by default (cached mode). Pass `--search` for live results or set `web_search = "live"` in config.

### Shell completions
```bash
codex completion bash
codex completion zsh
codex completion fish
```
Example for `~/.zshrc`:
```bash
eval "$(codex completion zsh)"
```

### Approval modes
- **Auto (default):** Read files, edit, and run commands within the working directory.
- **Read-only:** Browse files but requires approval for changes/commands.
- **Full Access:** Work across the machine, including network, without asking.

### Scripting Codex
Use `exec` for non-interactive runs:
```bash
codex exec "fix the CI failure"
```

### Working with Codex cloud
```bash
codex cloud exec --env ENV_ID "Summarize open bugs"
```

### Slash commands
Quick access to workflows like `/review`, `/fork`, or custom prompts.

### Prompt editor
Press `Ctrl + G` in the compos

bash
codex cloud exec --env ENV_ID "Summarize open bugs"
```

### Slash commands
Quick access to workflows like `/review`, `/fork`, or custom prompts.

### Prompt editor
Press `Ctrl + G` in the composer to open the editor defined by `VISUAL` or `EDITOR`.

### Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Connect Codex to tools by adding STDIO or streaming HTTP servers in `~/.codex/config.toml`.

### Tips and shortcuts
- `@` in composer for fuzzy file search.
- `Enter` while running to inject instructions; `Tab` to queue follow-up.
- `!` prefix for local shell commands (e.g., `!ls`).
- `Esc` twice to edit previous message.
- `--add-dir` to expose more writable roots.

---

## 2. Reference (Command Line Options)

### Global flags
| Key | Type / Values | Details |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `--add-dir` | path | Grant additional directories write access. |
| `--ask-for-approval, -a` | untrusted \| on-request \| never | Control when Codex pauses for approval. |
| `--cd, -C` | path | Set the working directory. |
| `--config, -c` | key=value | Override configuration values (JSON parsed). |
| `--yolo` | boolean | Bypass approvals and sandboxing. |
| `--disable` | feature | Force-disable a feature flag. |
| `--enable` | feature | Force-enable a feature flag. |
| `--full-auto` | boolean | Shortcut: `on-request` approval and `workspace-write` sandbox. |
| `--image, -i` | path[,path...] | Attach image files. |
| `--model, -m` | string | Override the model (e.g., `gpt-5-codex`). |
| `--no-alt-screen` | boolean | Disable TUI alternate screen mode. |
| `--oss` | boolean | Use local open source model provider (Ollama). |
| `--profile, -p` | string | Load a specific profile from `config.toml`. |
| `--sandbox, -s` | read-only \| workspace-write \| danger-full-access | Select sandbox policy. |
| `--search` | boolean | Enable live web search. |

### Command overview
- `codex`: Launch TUI.
- `codex app`: Launch macOS desktop app.
- `codex app-server`: Launch app server (experimental).
- `codex apply`: App

| boolean | Enable live web search. |

### Command overview
- `codex`: Launch TUI.
- `codex app`: Launch macOS desktop app.
- `codex app-server`: Launch app server (experimental).
- `codex apply`: Apply cloud task diff.
- `codex cloud`: Interact with cloud tasks.
- `codex completion`: Generate shell scripts.
- `codex exec`: Non-interactive run.
- `codex execpolicy`: Evaluate rule files.
- `codex features`: Manage feature flags.
- `codex fork`: Fork a session.
- `codex login`: Authenticate.
- `codex logout`: Remove credentials.
- `codex mcp`: Manage MCP servers.
- `codex mcp-server`: Run Codex as an MCP server.
- `codex resume`: Continue a session.
- `codex sandbox`: Run commands in Codex sandbox.

### Command details (Selected)
#### codex app-server
```bash
codex app-server --listen stdio://
codex app-server --listen ws://IP:PORT
```

#### codex exec
```bash
codex exec "fix the CI failure" --json --output-last-message ./result.md
```

#### codex mcp
```bash
codex mcp add my-server -- python3 server.py
codex mcp add remote-server --url https://api.example.com/mcp
```

#### codex sandbox
- **macOS Seatbelt:** `codex sandbox -- ls -la`
- **Linux Landlock:** `codex sandbox --full-auto -- ./build.sh`

---

## 3. Slash Commands

### Built-in slash commands
| Command | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- |
| `/permissions` | Set approval requirements (Auto, Read Only, Full Access). |
| `/sandbox-add-read-dir` | Grant sandbox read access to extra dir (Windows). |
| `/agent` | Switch active agent thread. |
| `/apps` | Browse and insert app connectors (e.g., `$github`). |
| `/clear` | Clear terminal and start fresh chat. |
| `/compact` | Summarize conversation to free tokens. |
| `/copy` | Copy latest completed output. |
| `/diff` | Show Git diff (including untracked). |
| `/exit` | Exit the CLI. |
| `/experimental` | Toggle experimental features (e.g., Subagents). |
| `/feedback` | Send logs/diagnostics to maintainers. |
| `/init` | Generate `AGENTS.md` scaffold. |
| `/logout` | Sign ou

CLI. |
| `/experimental` | Toggle experimental features (e.g., Subagents). |
| `/feedback` | Send logs/diagnostics to maintainers. |
| `/init` | Generate `AGENTS.md` scaffold. |
| `/logout` | Sign out. |
| `/mcp` | List available MCP tools. |
| `/mention` | Attach a file (e.g., `/mention src/main.ts`). |
| `/model` | Choose active model (e.g., `gpt-4.1-mini`). |
| `/fast` | Toggle Fast mode for GPT-5.4. |
| `/plan` | Switch to plan mode. |
| `/personality` | Choose style (friendly, pragmatic, none). |
| `/ps` | Show background terminals and output. |
| `/fork` | Fork current conversation. |
| `/resume` | Resume a saved conversation. |
| `/new` | Start new conversation in same session. |
| `/review` | Ask for a working tree review. |
| `/status` | Display config and token usage. |
| `/debug-config` | Print config layer diagnostics. |
| `/statusline` | Configure TUI footer items. |

### Key Workflows
#### Set model
Type `/model`, select from popup. Verify with `/status`.

#### Toggle Fast mode
```bash
/fast on
/fast off
/fast status
```

#### Plan mode
```bash
/plan "Propose a migration plan"
```

#### Debug Config
Type `/debug-config` to see `allowed_approval_policies`, `mcp_servers`, `rules`, etc.

#### Status Line
Type `/statusline` to toggle items: model, context, git branch, tokens, etc.

#### Compact
Type `/compact` to replace earlier turns with a summary.

#### Mention
Type `/mention` followed by path to highlight files for Codex.
